affordances.name: Model market pressure first
affordances.mechanism: When price, scarcity, substitution, or capacity constraints are central, use supply and demand as a first-pass model for market pressure before adding behavioral, regulatory, power, narrative, and threshold effects.

use_when: Price, scarcity, substitution, or capacity constraints are central to the situation.
A team needs a clean first-pass model of market pressure and quantity before adding nuance.
A business decision depends on whether price or supply changes will materially shift demand and profitability.
do_not_use_when: The model is being treated as a full explanation for human behavior.
Power, regulation, narrative, habit, anchoring, or critical-mass thresholds are doing most of the work.
The answer would turn an economic principle into a generic policy verdict without execution constraints.
case_evidence_needed: The relevant supply constraint or capacity limit.
The demand signal, substitution path, or willingness to buy.
The price, volume, or market-share lever under discussion.
Behavioral or institutional factors that may break the simple model.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to use supply and demand as a bounded first-pass model, then name the non-economic forces that could dominate the observed behavior.
evidence_required: The price, scarcity, substitution, or capacity constraint.
At least one check for behavioral, regulatory, narrative, power, or threshold effects.
good_output_shape: Names where the simple model may fail.
Adds a second-order or implementation check.

diagnostic_questions: Which lever is changing: supply, demand, price, substitution, or capacity?
What evidence shows this is a market-pressure problem rather than mostly habit, power, regulation, or narrative?
What second-order behavior follows if the price or supply condition changes?
misuse_guards: Do not treat market clearing as a complete policy answer.
Do not ignore constraints, anchors, habits, regulation, or thresholds.
Do not use supply and demand as the only model for human behavior.

source_evidence.source_quote: price, scarcity, substitution, or capacity constraints are central drivers
clean first-pass model of how market pressure, incentives, and quantity interact
power, regulation, narrative, or habit are doing most of the work

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 16 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports supply and demand as a bounded first-pass market-pressure model.
dropped_material: Did not promote supply and demand to a full human-behavior explanation.
Did not create market-clearing policy advice.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether this card helps distinguish market pressure from behavioral and institutional pressure.

absence_records.reason: The source supports supply and demand as a predictive market model, but it explicitly warns against treating it as a full explanation for human or institutional behavior.

source_evidence.source_quote: treated as a full explanation for human behavior

absence_records.reason: The source's activation conditions require price, scarcity, substitution, or capacity constraints; it does not support generic supply-demand claims without those constraints.

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 16 source reading found useful reviewed depth for market-pressure diagnosis with explicit behavioral caveats.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, pricing engine, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: A later packet should compare this against elasticity, price-discrimination, and critical-mass when all appear in the same packet.
